meta.title: Guangzhou
meta.subtitle: Wholesale-market map
meta.tagline: Guangzhou is not one market but dozens — a whole city of specialised wholesale complexes, the trading face of the Pearl River Delta. This is the map of where to buy what.
meta.methodology: Market coordinates are from Google Places, converted from China's GCJ-02 datum to WGS-84 to sit correctly on the OpenStreetMap / CARTO basemap. Each pin marks a market's dominant trade; large complexes carry more than one. Markets relocate, merge and close more often than factory towns, so confirm a market is still operating and at this address before a visit.

markets.note: South China's fabric hub — 20+ markets and thousands of stalls beside Sun Yat-sen University; trims and accessories too.
The flagship Zhongda building — best-quality fabric and an accessories floor (zips, buttons, lace); popular with overseas buyers.
The toy, gift and stationery centre; party and seasonal goods by the boxful.
Yiwu-style small commodities — toys, gifts, household goods and accessories under one roof.
The benchmark clothing wholesaler — mid-to-high women's fashion by the railway station.
The shoe-wholesale zone — buyers from Africa, Russia and the Middle East; shoes and shoe materials.
Watches and clocks wholesale, from movements to finished pieces.
Fashion jewelry and accessories — scarves, hats, hair, low-to-mid price by the piece in bulk.
Consumer electronics, phones, cameras and second-hand gear; repairs and trade-ins.
Cheap, fast women's fashion in dense narrow lanes; buyers from Africa and Southeast Asia.
Historic jade trading street — raw and finished jade, with on-site grading.
Guangzhou's largest jewelry, gemstone, pearl and bead wholesale complex.
Traditional Chinese medicine, herbs and dried seafood — a vast specialist market.
China's largest tea wholesale market — every region and grade, plus teaware.
Children's-wear and baby-goods wholesale cluster.
The Sanyuanli bag zone — dozens of markets for bags, wallets, belts and luggage.

markets.hanzi: 白云世界皮具贸易中心
markets.note: Flagship leather-goods mall in the Sanyuanli cluster; quality varies and replica trade is heavy.
Asia-Pacific's leading cosmetics and beauty wholesale market — products, packaging and salon machines.
Computers, phones and digital products (Pacific Digital, Tianhe Computer City).
High-volume, low-price casual clothing and jeans; the value end of Guangzhou fashion.

orientation.v: Zhongda (Haizhu) — South China's fabric hub, 20+ markets by Sun Yat-sen University.
Baima (mid-high), Shisanhang (cheap/trendy), Shahe (volume/casual) — three price tiers.
Sanyuanli / Guihuagang (Baiyun) — dozens of bag markets in one zone.
Hualin and Liwan Plaza (Liwan) for jade, pearls and beads; Taikang for fashion jewelry.
Yide Road and Onelink (Yuexiu), by Haizhu Square.
Zhanxi for shoes and watches, Fangcun for tea, Qingping for herbs, Gangding for digital.
Most open ~9:00–18:00; weekdays are calmer. Wholesale minimums apply, bargaining is expected, and you should confirm samples before any bulk order.

notes: Guangzhou is not one market but dozens — each a multi-floor complex, clustered by trade. The city is the trading face of the Pearl River Delta's factories.
Markets cluster by product: fabric at Zhongda, clothing by price tier (Baima, Shisanhang, Shahe), bags at Sanyuanli, jade in Liwan, gifts and toys at Yide.
Most stalls are wholesale-only with minimums; the real price needs a bulk order, and bargaining is expected — opening around 30–50% of the quote is normal.
Quality and intellectual-property vary sharply market to market; confirm samples first, and be aware that some markets (bags and watches especially) trade heavily in replicas.
